AI Halftime Advice

EXPLOIT

Consider attacking the space behind Switzerland’s midfield line with quicker vertical entries from the central connector, rather than continuing to recycle only around the block.

Evidence: SAO triplet at minute 84: Switzerland central midfielder executed a vertical line-breaking pass and created a chance; dashboard shows Switzerland’s middle-third tilt is 60.9, indicating central congestion that can be bypassed.

FIX

Consider tightening Argentina’s rest-defense against direct central passes, because the current compact block is still allowing one clean line break to become a chance.

Evidence: Sequence 2 vulnerability notes space for rapid switches, and the SAO outcome was chance_created after a vertical pass through Argentina’s midfield line.

FORMATION

Consider shifting Argentina’s right-sided outlet higher and closer to the central connector to create a more aggressive right-half-space triangle, instead of leaving the wide player isolated on the touchline.

Evidence: Argentina right-sided player spent 42 of 45 tracked frames on the right, while Argentina’s field tilt is heavily middle-weighted (54.1 middle vs 21.0 attacking), suggesting width is present but not yet connected into penetration.

PERSONNEL

Consider giving Switzerland’s right-sided player a clearer outlet role in build-up, because the current low-movement profile suggests he is staying safe rather than stretching Argentina’s block.

Evidence: Switzerland right-sided player: 45 of 47 frames in the right zone, only 20.54 px/s average speed and 472.3 px total distance; the sequence notes say Switzerland are attempting to stretch Argentina horizontally but also remain compact.
